sanskrit:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः।
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न हि उपपद्यते॥
transliteration:
etan me saṃśayaṃ kṛṣṇa chettum arhasy aśeṣataḥ |
tvad-anyaḥ saṃśayasya-asya chettā na hi upapadyate ||
literal_meaning: This my doubt, O Kṛṣṇa, to cut you are fit completely; other than You of this doubt a cutter indeed is not to be found.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna admits he is stuck in a loop of uncertainty about the fate of a yogi who fails. He externalizes the solution, insisting only Kṛṣṇa can terminate the rumination, revealing transference of agency and a reluctance to own the cognitive dissonance.
psychological_state: Catastrophic rumination and absolutist thinking (“only You can fix this”) typical of high-functioning performance anxiety; uses idealization of the therapist/God to avoid confronting internal ambiguity.
trigger_scenario: A top-tier student about to attempt the bar exam keeps asking the mentor “What if I fail?” and believes no one except this mentor can calm the panic.
anchor_text: Hand your doubt to awareness; let it dissolve there.
